raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Senate Bill 1412 aimed to close a loophole in state regulations to protect the integrity of the curriculum development process for K-12 public school curriculums.",
    "SB 1412 proposed to prohibit individuals who profit from the sale of curriculum or professional development from serving on the Instructional Quality Commission’s Curriculum Framework and Evaluation Criteria Committees.",
    "Existing regulations provide conflict-of-interest protections for grades K-8, which SB 1412 sought to expand to grades 9-12.",
    "The veto of Senate Bill 1412 is viewed as a setback to bringing the conflict-of-interest portion of the Education Code into the 21st century.",
    "Senate Bill 1412 focused broadly on curriculum vendors and aimed to increase public confidence in the ethical standards of K-12 curriculum creation and implementation."
  ]
}
```
